channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Syrian military launches counterattacks in an attempt to halt insurgents' surprise advance
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian military rushed reinforcements to the country's northwest and launched airstrikes on a major city Sunday in an attempt to push back insurgents who seized the country's largest city of Aleppo in a surprise offensive in recent days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Heavy snow and numbing temperatures keep parts of the US in a deep freeze
item.description: LOWVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Heavy snowfall and numbing temperatures kept parts of the U.S. in a deep freeze Sunday as the Thanksgiving holiday weekend draws to a close.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA and CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Heavy snow and numbing temperatures keep parts of the US in a deep freeze
item.description: Heavy snowfall and numbing temperatures kept parts of the U.S. in a deep freeze Sunday as the Thanksgiving holiday weekend draws to a close.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DACA recipients worry their protection from deportation won't last another Trump term
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Reyna Montoya was 10 when she and her family fled violence in Tijuana and illegally immigrated to the U.S. Growing up in Arizona, she worried even a minor traffic violation could lead to her deportation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What the cost-of-living adjustment increase in Social Security means
item.description: A 2.5% cost-of-living adjustment increase will mean an extra $50 a month for most Social Security and SSI recipients.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Romanians vote in parliamentary election as turmoil in presidential race grips the nation
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanians cast ballots Sunday in a parliamentary election sandwiched between a two-round presidential race, the first of which has plunged the European Union and NATO member into unprecedented turmoil following allegations of electoral violations and Russian interference.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lou Carnesecca, Hall of Fame coach who led St. John's for 24 seasons, dies
item.description: The excitable St. John’s coach whose outlandish sweaters became an emblem of his team’s rousing Final Four run in 1985, died at 99 on Saturday, just a few weeks shy of his 100th birthday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security reforms face uncertain future in Senate
item.description: A House-passed bill to reform aspects of Social Security is lingering in the Senate as questions bubble up over its path forward.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Don't miss these holiday shipping deadlines
item.description: The peak holiday shopping – and shipping – season is underway. If you want to make sure your gifts make it under the tree in time, you'll need to plan ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of chickpeas through California's Death Valley National Park – the driest place in North America – burst into flames last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in national park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cancel unused credit cards or keep them? Here are pros and cons
item.description: Keeping an account open carries some benefits, but closing a credit card may make sense in some scenarios.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz saga moves toward final act in House
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz’s (R-Fla.) time in Congress may be over, but the saga over the House Ethics Committee’s report on its years-long investigation into him is in its final act.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hero Girl Scout saves dad after he suffered heart attack in Virginia
item.description: Vada Carawan's Virginia Beach Girl Scout troop had just learned CPR 11 days earlier, and in that moment last Saturday, with her father unresponsive and her dog upset, she channeled her energies into saving him.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, ABC 4

item.title: Canada's Trudeau returns home after Trump meeting without assurances that tariffs are off the table
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is returning home after his meeting with Donald Trump without assurances the president-elect will back away from threatened tariffs on all products from the major American trading partner.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies And Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Earl Holliman, 'The Rainmaker' and 'Twilight Zone' actor, dead at 96
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Award-winning actor Earl Holliman has died at the age of 96, according to multiple reports. Holliman died Monday in hospice care at his home in Studio City according to his spouse, Craig Curtis, who told The Hollywood Reporter.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SF burglary victim’s medical device returned after GPS tracker leads to suspect
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- A victim of a vehicle burglary in San Francisco earlier this month had her luggage and medical equipment returned after a GPS tracker led police to the suspect, according to the San Francisco Police Department. On Nov.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers extend contract of postseason hero
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers have agreed to a contract extension with one of the key pieces of the team's World Series title run. The Dodgers reached agreement Friday on a deal with Tommy Edman, ensuring that the do-it-all utility-man will remain with the team for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Most Americans worried about affording gifts this year
item.description: Most Americans are worried about affording gifts this year but that doesn't mean they're cutting back on spending.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Lake-effect snow heading for New York state could disrupt travel and football
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — Black Friday was giving way to a white weekend in parts of New York state, with the first big snow of the season threatening to bury towns along lakes Erie and Ontario during a hectic holiday travel and shopping weekend.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First popularly elected Black mayor in New England, Thirman Milner, has died at 91
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — Former Hartford Mayor Thirman Milner, the first popularly elected Black mayor in New England, has died, the Connecticut NAACP said on Friday. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexico's congress votes to charge cruise ship passengers $42 per head for port calls
item.description: Cruise ship industry players are up in arms after the lower house of Mexico’s Congress voted this week to charge a $42 immigration levy for every passenger on cruise ships that dock in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 Tren de Aragua members tied to viral Aurora video arrested in NYC
item.description: Two Venezuelan gang members who are allegedly tied to a video at a Colorado apartment complex have been arrested.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus after disastrous Thanksgiving loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Head of Ohio's teacher pension fund retires Sunday, saying fund is strong despite staff vacancies
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The outgoing head of Ohio's troubled teachers retirement system said that she is leaving the fund in strong fiscal condition, despite the turmoil at the top that her successor will inherit.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Colorado officer uses kid's bike during pursuit
item.description: A Lone Tree police officer went above and beyond to try and catch a theft suspect who fled on a scooter in a neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, KRON4

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect accused in dad's camping murder 'cut off' from self-defense claim after backtracking on confession
item.description: The suspect in the murder of a Montana dad and avid outdoorsman pleaded not guilty Tuesday after allegedly claiming self-defense in his confession to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Northeast hit with rain, snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities
item.description: The U.S. Northeast has been hit with a winter storm that has brought rain and snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities and disrupted travel plans for those moving around on the holiday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
